title: The suburb dubbed Britain's 'crazy vape shop capital'

input.lead: Traders say the Manchester vape market is the biggest in Europe, with shops selling wholesale
input.summary: Traders say the Manchester vape market is the biggest in Europe, with shops selling wholesale
input.evidence_text: The suburb dubbed Britain's 'crazy vape shop capital' - BBC News Image caption, The vape district is crowded with large adverts By Stewart Whittingham North West Published 51 minutes ago An inner-city part of Manchester that was once a bustling hub for counterfeit goods before a major police crackdown has now emerged as what traders have described as "Britain's vape capital". Two narrow streets in Cheetham Hill are now filled with bright, colourful shopfronts with names such as Vaporesso, Xhale and The Vape Boyz. There are 51 shops selling vapes on Harris Street and Overbridge Road, something residents have said is "crazy", while a government minister has admitted it is "bizarre". The stores, which sell wholesale to customers coming from as far away as Glasgow and Edinburgh, emerged after 200 shops in the area were shut down in a two-year police operation to tackle the fake clothes trade and drug dealing. Image source, GMP Image caption, Police carried out a series of raids as part of Operation Vulcan The area had been described as the UK's counterfeit capital, with hundreds of millions of pounds worth of bogus goods seized by police as part of Operation Vulcan. The crackdown saw Greater Manchester Police (GMP) make nearly 240 arrests and seize 1,050 tonnes of counterfeit items. In the years since, vape supermarkets filled with stock and large billboards advertising their wares have emerged in the area, which is near Strangeways prison. 'Dwarfs everything' Kamran Akhtar, manager at The Vape Boyz on Harris Street, said: "It's pretty mad how many vape shops there are here. "We have been here for a while but there have never been so many as there are now. "We sell wholesale to other shops and businesses. People come from all over the North to buy here - from Bradford, Live

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Diecast neighbours tell of 'constant anxiety' over venue noise

input.lead: Diecast has now been asked to close the beer garden early after years of complaints over noise
input.summary: Diecast has now been asked to close the beer garden early after years of complaints over noise
input.evidence_text: Diecast neighbours tell of 'constant anxiety' over venue noise - BBC News Image source, Maged Selim Image caption, Maged Selim, 62, and wife Meena Shah, 65 say the noise from the venue is "inescapable" By Georgie Docker , North West and Declan Carey , Local Democracy Reporting Service Published 49 minutes ago Residents living next to a popular venue in Manchester have said they live with "constant anxiety" because of loud noise, anti-social crowds, and even public urination. Diecast, an event and social space which opened near Manchester Piccadilly Station in 2023, has a capacity for thousands of revellers. "It's constant anxiety," said Maged Selim, who has lived in the area almost 20 years. "Shouting and screaming all the time. And that low level drumming of the bass, it gets into your head." Diecast told a council meeting last week it had spent money trying to reduce noise, but several licensing conditions were put in place, including closing the beer garden at 23:00. Diecast has a late night club space as well as beer and food halls. Maged Selim, 62, and his wife Meena Shah, 65, first spoke to the BBC in 2024 about issues at the former metalworks factory venue , and they said their situation had not improved since. "Every weekend, and often on Wednesdays and Thursdays, it is not a matter of if the bass will start, it's a matter of when," Selim said. "The thing about low level bass is it's not loud, it gets into your head. "You just can't concentrate on anything you're doing." Image caption, Diecast has now been told to close the beer garden by 23:00 Selim added: "And then there is the crowd noise. "People are just shouting and screaming from the beer garden just all the time. "You turn the TV up or the radio on but it is the vibration - it stresses you out all evenin

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Footie fan 'pushed to limit' in challenge for Wolverhampton charities

input.lead: Tom Wilmott has run a mile for every goal scored in the World Cup to raise money for two charities
input.summary: Tom Wilmott has run a mile for every goal scored in the World Cup to raise money for two charities
input.evidence_text: A football fan who has been running a mile for every goal scored during the World Cup said his "crazy" challenge has pushed him to the limit, but it has been worth it Tom Wilmott has so far run a total of 322 miles since the tournament started on 11 June, to raise money for two Wolverhampton charities He has remained injury-free, even through the peak of six matches a day at the end of the group stage saw him run two lots of 19 miles, 16 and then 20 miles back-to- back during the heatwave "The support has been amazing," he said. "The messages, people's stories and [people said] they find it really inspirational." Wilmott, from Manchester, has passed his target of £2,000 and said he was hoping to "kick on" and reach £3,000 for Brain Tumour Support and Compton Care hospice "I've raised £2,840 so far which is great. I'm really grateful for everyone that has donated so far," he said

title: Cake freebie request from The Voice 'abysmal', says bakery

input.lead: The company behind The Voice UK apologises after asking bakery Get Baked for free food
input.summary: The company behind The Voice UK apologises after asking bakery Get Baked for free food
input.evidence_text: An independent bakery owner has said it was a "kick in the teeth" to be asked by The Voice UK to provide the ITV show's guests and judges with free cakes Rich Myers, 37, from Get Baked, which has its flagship store in Manchester, described the request as a "total disgrace", adding that large companies should not ask small businesses to supply products in return for so-called "exposure" Myers shared the email on Instagram, which said ITV was unable to pay but the food would be seen by "highly influential" coaches, presenters and contestants Lifted Entertainment, which makes The Voice UK for ITV, apologised and said "steps had been taken to ensure this type of request is never made again" In the email, The Voice staff asked whether Get Baked, which has its production bakery in Leeds, could supply products for talent dressing rooms and green rooms during filming for no payment It said ITV would be "unable to offer anything monetary or otherwise in return"

title: Wildfires destroy moorland size of 1,250 football pitches

input.lead: Hundreds of hectares of "precious" moorland have been burned by wildfires, United Utilities says
input.summary: Hundreds of hectares of "precious" moorland have been burned by wildfires, United Utilities says
input.evidence_text: Wildfires across Greater Manchester and Derbyshire have burned hundreds of hectares of moorland equivalent to the size of 1,250 football pitches, the landowner has said Firefighters are warning "people must stay away" from the area, adding that they had already seen visitors lighting barbecues there this weekend More than 100 firefighters and volunteers are fighting the blaze on two fronts at Dove Stone, after the fire in Saddleworth merged with a pre-existing fire at Tintwistle , and at Swineshaw Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) said: "People must stay away from affected moorland areas and must not light BBQs, campfires or any other open flames." Landowner United Utilities (UU) said: "At present, the fires have burnt a total of 885 hectares of precious moorland, equivalent to 1,250 football pitches." A spokesperson said the firm continued to work with GMFRS, Greater Manchester Police, Derbyshire Fire and Rescue, the RSPB and Mountain Rescue "to tackle wildfires at Dovestone and Tintwistle"

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July

title: 'I didn't think I'd be graduating' after Freshers' Week cancer op

input.lead: Ella Tyman earns a first-class honours degree following cancer treatment after arriving at university
input.summary: Ella Tyman earns a first-class honours degree following cancer treatment after arriving at university
input.evidence_text: Ella Tyman had just moved into university accommodation in the autumn of 2023, and was about to enjoy Freshers' Week, when she was told she was needed urgent surgery for cancer The 21-year-old, of Skelmersdale, Lancashire, had been diagnosed with parotid gland cancer after doctors found a lump behind her ear during an appointment for an eye infection Ella said she had believed the cancer could be quickly removed before returning to life at the University of Salford. But a planned 45-minute procedure became a six-hour operation after the cancer had doubled in size and wrapped around facial nerves "I didn't think I'd be graduating now," she said, having achieved a first-class honours degree "They had to cut the nerves in my face," she said "I didn't even want to walk out of my house, let alone go to university."

title: Man found guilty of murdering boy who stole e-bike in Middleton

input.lead: Terrence King, 60, is convicted of killing 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the bike
input.summary: Terrence King, 60, is convicted of killing 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the bike
input.evidence_text: A man has been found guilty of murdering a teenager who stole an e-bike by deliberately driving his car into him Terrence King, 60, killed 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the £3,000 bike, from another boy in Middleton in Greater Manchester on 22 July 2025 The jury was told King had admitted manslaughter and accepted his actions caused Devon's death, but he denied murder But he was found guilty of murder following a two-week trial and will be sentenced on 23 July at Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester There were screams of joy from the victim's family and friends inside the courtroom as the guilty verdict was announced along with shouts of "thank you" CCTV and doorbell camera footage showed Simmonds-Caines riding the e-bike, which the court heard he - along with a friend - had stolen from another boy on the Langley Estate

title: Two more arson arrests over moorland fire at Dove Stone Reservoir

input.lead: Three people have now been arrested in connection with the fire, which broke out on Saturday
input.summary: Three people have now been arrested in connection with the fire, which broke out on Saturday
input.evidence_text: Two more people have been arrested on suspicion of arson after a fire broke out on a Greater Manchester moorland The blaze started last Saturday at Dove Stone Reservoir in Greenfield, Saddleworth, and is still burning, with smoke being carried across the region Great Manchester Police said a man in his 20s was arrested earlier on suspicion of arson, while a woman was arrested on Thursday on the same suspicion. The woman has been released on bail The arrests follow the charging of Shania Care-Slede, 20, of Market Street, Hyde, with aggravated arson, being reckless as to whether life was endangered, and dangerous driving She is due to appear at Manchester Crown Court on 18 August "This investigation has been progressing at pace. We are nearly a week on and the fire at Dove Stone is still ongoing, and it has had devastating effects on the reservoir," said Det Insp Andrew Day

The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

input.evidence_text: Ella Tyman had just moved into university accommodation in the autumn of 2023, and was about to enjoy Freshers' Week, when she was told she was needed urgent surgery for cancer The 21-year-old, of Skelmersdale, Lancashire, had been diagnosed with parotid gland cancer after doctors found a lump behind her ear during an appointment for an eye infection Ella said she had believed the cancer could be quickly removed before returning to life at the University of Salford. But a planned 45-minute procedure became a six-hour operation after the cancer had doubled in size and wrapped around facial nerves "I didn't think I'd be graduating now," she said, having achieved a first-class honours degree "They had to cut the nerves in my face," she said "I didn't even want to walk out of my house, let alone go to university."

input.summary: A moorland blaze which led to the closure of a major road linking South Yorkshire and Greater Manchester was started by a crashed car bursting into flames Firefighters were called to the A628 Woodhead Pass near Flouch at about 13:30 BST on Thursday to reports a car had come off t
input.evidence_text: A moorland blaze which led to the closure of a major road linking South Yorkshire and Greater Manchester was started by a crashed car bursting into flames Firefighters were called to the A628 Woodhead Pass near Flouch at about 13:30 BST on Thursday to reports a car had come off the road and set on fire Fire station manager Jonathan Poynter said the flames moved quickly due to the dry ground, and required "copious amounts of water" to put them out "At its peak, it wasn't so much about the flame height and the head of the fire, but due to the nature of the land and the peatiness, it can get deep really quick, so that was the main concern for the crews," he said Poynter said crews left the scene at about 23:00 BST, with periodic checks carried out throughout the evening. No injuries were reported Firefighters returned on Friday morning to continue damping down, while a drone was used to carry out thermal imaging of the site

But it's not just the object itself which is so special, it's also the words that are written on it Dr Penner, a curator at the John Rylands Library , is talking about a remarkable book known as the Manchester Odyssey. Created in Ancient Egypt 1,700 years ago, it is the world's oldest copy of Homer's epic poem And the artefact has helped inspire this summer's biggest Hollywood blockbuster . As Christopher Nolan 's star-studded, Oscar-tipped retelling of the homecoming tale prepares to hit the cinemas this weekend, attention is once again turning to Manchester's unique connection to Anc "It's incredibly old, but there's something very modern about it," says Jeremy, ahead of the manuscript going on public display for the very first time. "Here we are looking at this object in the future but it also takes you back to the past "That's what I love about it - it's so evocative."

title: The empty housing estate in a corner of Salford

input.lead: Salford The empty housing estate in a corner of Salford Social housing has been left empty for months while thousands of people wait for new homes.
input.summary: Salford The empty housing estate in a corner of Salford Social housing has been left empty for months while thousands of people wait for new homes. What's going on?
input.evidence_text: Salford The empty housing estate in a corner of Salford Social housing has been left empty for months while thousands of people wait for new homes. What's going on? Comments News Neal Keeling Chief reporter and Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Reporter In the hot afternoon sun, Longshaw Drive feels like it could be the setting of a Western shootout. The streets of the Salford housing estate are empty, most of the homes are shuttered up. If two cowboys turned up on the dusty tarmac, you'd barely raise an eyebrow The ‘new neighbourhood’ of 177 homes in Little Hulton was supposed to be complete and ready to move in months ago. But while the beautiful terraced homes look ready to live in, only around a dozen are actually occupied “Well, it’s nice and quiet,” said one neighbour, who moved into the empty estate in September. “Though it is a bit creepy. We don’t know what’s going on, really.” The housing estate was hailed as one part of Salford council’s ‘largest social housing project in 50 years’ when it was first conceived Exactly 93 of the homes are for social housing, with a further 20 for shared ownership to help people get on the housing ladder, and 42 council-owned homes for sale at private market

Once owned by the late Jimmy 'The Weed' Donnelly , a member of Manchester's notorious Quality Street Gang, in 2016 the pub became the focus of a high-profile mur Mr Joyce, then 29, of Gorton , was last seen at the Pollard Street pub in September 1999. Seventeen years later, police dug up the cellar of the Bank of England after being tipped off that Mr Joyce's body was buried there It came after GMP received 'credible' information he had been killed during a fight. But, the Manchester Evening News revealed, the force had been informed three years earlier about the possibility that Mr Joyce's body was in the pub - and not acted Nothing was found during the two week police excavation at the property. The dad-of-one's disappearance remains unsolved and the case is with GMP's cold case unit

But it's not just the object itself which is so special, it's also the words that are written on it Dr Penner, a curator at the John Rylands Library , is talking about a remarkable book known as the Manchester Odyssey. Created in Ancient Egypt 1,700 years ago, it is the world's oldest copy of Homer's epic poem And the artefact has helped inspire this summer's biggest Hollywood blockbuster . As Christopher Nolan 's star-studded, Oscar-tipped retelling of the homecoming tale prepares to hit the cinemas this weekend, attention is once again turning to Manchester's unique connection to Anc "It's incredibly old, but there's something very modern about it," says Jeremy, ahead of the manuscript going on public display for the very first time. "Here we are looking at this object in the future but it also takes you back to the past "That's what I love about it - it's so evocative."

input.evidence_text: Salford The empty housing estate in a corner of Salford Social housing has been left empty for months while thousands of people wait for new homes. What's going on? Comments News Neal Keeling Chief reporter and Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Reporter In the hot afternoon sun, Longshaw Drive feels like it could be the setting of a Western shootout. Created in Ancient Egypt 1,700 years ago, it is the world's oldest copy of Homer's epic poem And the artefact has helped inspire this summer's biggest Hollywood blockbuster . As Christopher Nolan 's star-studded, Oscar-tipped retelling of the homecoming tale prepares to hit the cinemas this weekend, attention is once again turning to Manchester's unique connection to Anc "It's incredibly old, but there's something very modern about it," says Jeremy, ahead of the manuscript going on public display for the very first time. title: 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency'

input.lead: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second.
input.summary: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging
input.evidence_text: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging over Manchester city centre and surrounding towns caused by the moorland will clear. But for the wildlife living on the scorched earth near Dovestone Reservoir in Oldham and on Tintwistle Moor near Glossop the impact has been devastating The moors are home to a rich variety of bird species. Meadow pipits flit, blackcaps move shyly and buzzards soar. The sound of the skylark's life-enhancing rhapsody rings out on both moors and the rare rock-loving Ring Ouzel known as the "mountain blackbird" breeds in the Peak Di But experts say the fires have caused "mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency". Scarred by walls of flames it will take years for the land to recover One woman has been remanded in custody after being charged with arson in connection with the moorland fire near Dovestone reservoir. Shania Care-Slede, 20, appeared at Manchester Magistrates' Court on July 14 after being charged with two offences View 11 Images Moorland fires raged for days above Dovestone Reservoir , Saddleworth in Oldham . Helicopters are used water from the reservoir to dampen the fire . 12 July 2026 (Image: Sean Hansford | Manchester Evening News)

input.evidence_text: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. The local The idea to build a 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could end the ‘frustration of traffic congestion’ plaguing one corner of Greater Manchester The Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead. The scheme, named ‘Trans-Pennine Connect’, could cut travel times between the two cities by 30 minutes and The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 Reducing congestion around the Mottram area of Tameside has been a long-held ambition of the local MP Jonathan Reynolds. He has successfully campaigned for the A57 link road scheme that is currently under construction This tunnel plan, if it ever gets the go ahead, would connect to the link road scheme that is due to be completed around 2030. Mr Reynolds has welcomed the idea that could benefit motorists coming through his patch

title: The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way

input.lead: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.summary: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felled by a Taser, the balaclava-clad figure rolled onto his back and raised a 9mm pistol Both hands wrapped around the grip he aimed the loaded gun straight at the police officer stood just a few feet away. "I could see the barrel pointing right at me and he was following my movements as I was trying to get away from him," Pc Dan Clayton would later recall "In that moment I was thinking of my pregnant wife and how she might have to bring up our child alone." Despite having his life flash before his eyes, the brave GMP cop remained remarkably calm. 'Gun, gun, gun' he shouted as he warned off a council parking warden who was trying to intervene And as he took shelter behind a parked car he also memorised a comprehensive description of the gunman, which just 24 hours later, would help lead to his capture. Pc Clayton's actions have now seen him presented with the Salford Bravery Award

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

input.summary: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir Strong winds have carried smoke across parts of Greater Manchester, including Manchester Residents should avoid areas affected by smoke wherever possible. If smoke is visible in your area, stay indoors with doors and windows closed. This is the most effective way to reduce exposure to wildfire smoke Wildfire smoke can irritate the eyes, nose, throat and lungs. Most healthy people will only experience temporary symptoms, but smoke can have a greater impact on babies and young children, older people, pregnant people, and those with existing heart or lung conditions For most people any symptoms will be temporary and will quickly disappear once you are away from the smoke. However, if symptoms persist or get worse, you should seek health advice Symptoms may include irritated eyes and airways, coughing, wheezing, shortness of breath and chest discomfort. If you have an asthma action plan or other agreed health management plan, follow it and make sure you have access to any prescribed inhalers or medication. If you don't

title: Oldham bucks national trend as more young people choose education and training

input.lead: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.summary: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.evidence_text: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET) The latest Department for Education (DfE) Local Authority Participation and NEET figures for the 2025/26 academic year show that 93.2 per cent of Oldham's 16 and 17-year-olds are participating in education and training, an increase of 2.8 percentage points on the previous year. T At the same time, Oldham's combined NEET and Not Known (NK) rate has fallen to 4.6%, down 1.1 percentage points from 2024/25. This is significantly lower than both the North West average of 5.5% and the England average of 5.8% The figures also highlight Oldham's strong performance, in partnership with the charity Positive Steps, in tracking and supporting young people Positive Steps provides young people with support, careers advice and guidance after school, or between 19 and 25 if they have an Education Healthcare Plan (EHCP), helping them to move into education, training or employment Only 59 young people out of 7,177 are currently unaccounted for – 0.8 per cent of the borough's young people. This is well below the North West average of 1.3 per cent and the England average of 2.4 per cent

title: Professor Brian Cox launches new partnership to inspire next generation of scientists in Oldham

input.evidence_text: Oldham Council’s Director of Growth James Kington welcomed the Active Science in Schools Project as an investment in the borough’s future workforce Chadderton-born physicist Professor Brian Cox returned to his home town for the launch of a partnership between Oldham Athletic Community Trust and Pharmaceutical Company AstraZeneca, which will help bring science to life for hundreds of primary school pupils in the borough Professor Cox launched the Active Science in Schools Project at Boundary Park’s event centre, where earlier in the day he met with local school children and discussed the importance of inspiring young people to pursue STEM (science, technology, engineering and maths) subjects The programme, delivered by Oldham Athletic Community Trust in partnership with biopharmaceutical company AstraZeneca, combines physical activity with scientific learning to make STEM subjects more engaging and accessible for children across Oldham Professor Cox joined a panel discussion alongside Oldham Council's Director of Growth James Kington, AstraZeneca UK Chair Shaun Grady, Oldham Athletic Community Trust Chief Executive Sandy Mitchell and Dr Lynne Bianchi, the founder of The Great Science Share for Schools, an annua Speaking at the launch, James highlighted the long-term benefits the project could bring to both young people and the local economy, along with Northern Roots project to create the UK's largest urban farm and eco-park and the SportsTown sports, education and wellbeing campus at B

title: Pupils with SEND inspired to aim high at Rochdale careers event - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale
input.summary: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale
input.evidence_text: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale This follows a careers event organised by Rochdale’s additional needs service (RANS), part of Rochdale Borough Council, at Middleton Arena to inspire the career aspirations of local pupils aged 10-14 with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) During a day packed full of careers advice, more than 60 pupils from nearby schools had the opportunity to hear from a number of successful professionals with additional needs This included 2-time Paralympic gold medallist and double amputee runner, Richard Whitehead MBE, who also holds the world record for the fastest marathon by a leg amputee This event came as national figures highlight only half the number of people in the UK with a disability aged 16-64 are in employment (52.8%), compared to 4 out of 5 people (82.5%) who don’t have a disability Local services have been working hard however, to address this challenge by highlighting the successful careers and futures that young people can achieve with a disability as they grow up

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. It seeks to capture the essence of sensations you get in your stomach that are so hard to verbalise, but that we all know and feel.” Kate Partington’s practice explores the subtleties of touch, connection, and the spaces that exist between bodies She is a mixed media artist working across analogue and digital spaces, constructing images that hover between materiality and emotion Her work examines how intimacy can be both tender and elusive, how connection can be felt rather than seen

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. With a capacity of over 53,000, state-of-the-art facilities, and a great atmosphere on matchdays, the Etihad Stadium is not only the club’s home but also a key part of Manchester’s sporting and cultural id

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. With a capacity of over 53,000, state-of-the-art facilities, and a great atmosphere on matchdays, the Etihad Stadium is not only the club’s home but also a key part of Manchester’s sporting and cultural id

input.evidence_text: She’s hilarious, honest, and completely unfiltered – and now she’s hitting the road Join GK Barry this September as she celebrates the release of her new book, It’s Giving Life , with an unmissable live tour packed full of laughs, oversharing, and some life advice (taken with a very big pinch of salt) From TikTok fame to becoming a breakout star on I’m a Celebrity…Get Me Out of Here! and a regular panelist on Loose Women , GK Barry has become one of the most influential and relatable young voices in British media. With over 5 million followers across social media and a chart-t Now, she’s bringing that same energy to the stage Expect outrageous stories, brutally honest confessions, and the kind of chaotic commentary that made her a household name. From dating disasters and digital drama to body image, sexuality, personal traumas and all the questionable decisions in between, nothing is off-limits. This This intimate evening is likely to sell out, so do not miss your chance for a hilarious night out and book today!

Having started comedy in 2016 Mike has amassed a large online following from podcast and stand-up clips, regularly getting millions of views on various platforms Mike began stand-up comedy in Chicago, where within two years he was working regularly at the most prestigious clubs in the city, including The Laugh Factory. He has since returned home because his mother made him, and has been quickly rising in the Irish and European scene, play Mike brought his first solo show The Best Boy in Ireland to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2018, in what was a sold-out run, and performed that same show to sold-out rooms at the Perth Fringe Festival in 2019 As well as co-hosting Mike & Vittorio’s Guide to Parenting with Vittorio Angelone, and Big Mike and The Chief with Rob Moriarty, Mike has appeared multiple times on smash hit podcast Have a Word and released his first comedy special in October 2023

title: Tech Leader Talks - Build, Buy or Rebuild at Scale | Manchester Digital

input.lead: Manchester Digital hosted a lunchtime Tech Leader Talks on 11th June 2026, which was chaired by Pete Farrell, Technology Director at Autotrader.
input.summary: Manchester Digital hosted a lunchtime Tech Leader Talks on 11th June 2026, which was chaired by Pete Farrell, Technology Director at Autotrader. The panel topic was ‘Build, Buy or Rebuild at Scale’, which explored the ongoing challenges for engineers and tech leaders
input.evidence_text: Manchester Digital hosted a lunchtime Tech Leader Talks on 11th June 2026, which was chaired by Pete Farrell, Technology Director at Autotrader. The panel topic was ‘Build, Buy or Rebuild at Scale’, which explored the ongoing challenges for engineers and tech leaders. The panel b A key theme throughout the discussion was recognising the signals that it is time to move on from legacy software and to modernise tech stacks. Richard said the trigger often comes from customers rather than technology itself. At Lloyds Banking Group (LBG), a move to Google Cloud Mariam pointed to developer experience and security as critical drivers for change. Systems built on older frameworks, such as early Ruby on Rails stacks, can become difficult to maintain or secure over time. She explained, “You don’t want to be running systems with outdated pack Chris explained that with Street being a relatively new business, their challenge is different - they don’t have legacy tech but they are scaling at such a pace that growth is doubling or tripling every year. The two completed their merger last week, creating a new London-based production The huge merger followed hot on the heels of the proposed Sky-ITV deal, which could redraw the UK TV landscape with the creation of a new business with hundreds of thousands of hours of programming and almost 200 production companies and labels nationwide Purple was a part of the previously All3-owned Objective Media Group, while All3 itself was taken over last year by Abu Dhabi and New York-based JV RedBird IMI. IMI, the Abu Dhabi-based side of the venture and it’s main funder, told Prolific North at the time of the takeover that Announcing the closure on social media, Purple creative director Pam Cavannagh said: “Sad to say goodbye to Purple. title: An acclaimed rock opera set in the aftermath of the Peterloo Massacre comes to Manchester this month

input.lead: An acclaimed rock opera set in the aftermath of the Peterloo Massacre comes to Manchester later this month Fusing progressive folk rock, classic rock, indie and blues, The Reformers’ Peterloo in Rock takes place immediately after the events of 1819, guiding the audience through p

input.evidence_text: An acclaimed rock opera set in the aftermath of the Peterloo Massacre comes to Manchester later this month Fusing progressive folk rock, classic rock, indie and blues, The Reformers’ Peterloo in Rock takes place immediately after the events of 1819, guiding the audience through pivotal reforms of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries and culminating in the present day The principal writer and composer, Robert (Bob) Carter, explains that the early reformers were looking for many things that have now been changed, such as fairer distribution of parliamentary seats, a voice for the working people of the north, universal suffrage, including a vote Carter also goes on to poignantly highlight that this struggle for equality persists as an issue today, a theme that resonates through the music The show features a striking complementary visual presentation created by Manchester graphic novelist Polyp ( Peterloo , Witnesses to a Massacre; Courage ), who has contributed to a fully immersive audio-visual experience Following a run of sold-out shows last autumn, Peterloo in Rock comes to the Mechanics Institute in Manchester on Sat 25 Jul. Tickets and more information can be found below; use the code HISTORIES 2026 to claim an exclusive discount

input.evidence_text: Stan, the famous Tyrannosaurus rex that has been on display in Manchester Museum’s Fossils Gallery for more than 20 years, will be heading to Victoria Baths next year The iconic dinosaur, a full-size cast made from one of the most complete Tyrannosaurus rex fossils ever found, will be on display in Victoria Baths’ Gala Pool from Mon 22 Mar 2027 – Mon 19 Apr 2027, marking the first time it has been removed from display since arriving at Manches Named after Stan Sacrison, the avocational palaeontologist who discovered it, Stan was excavated in South Dakota in 1992 and has since been cast many times. Estimated to be 67 million years old, the original specimen is on display at the Natural History Museum Abu Dhabi The process of moving him to Victoria Baths will be a gargantuan task that will take a team of specialists a full week. At 12 metres long and 3.7 metres tall, Stan is roughly the size of a a double-decker bus, and its bones must be dismantled and reassembled in a strict order to During the residency, Stan will be on display to the public but will also be the focal point for a full programme of events, details of which will be announced soon. Manchester Museum is also working hard behind-the-scenes on a temporary Stan stand-in, with news on that to be ann Georgina Young, Acting Director at Manchester Museum, said: “Manchester Museum cares for around 4.5m objects and taking them to unexpected places creates new opportunities for learning and connection. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . input.evidence_text: Fancy a space to connect with the community away from Canal Street? Kiki Family Social is a relaxed, grassroots meet-up with no rigid expectations, pressure, or hierarchy. The space is shaped by the people who come along — everyone helps make it what it is We meet every Thursday, and there’s always space at the table whether you’re new to Manchester or just dipping your toe into social meet-ups You’ll usually find a mix of familiar faces and new ones each week. We typically gather at a reserved table inside with pride flags, and there’s often a table outside too. On busy nights we’ll make sure flags are on the outside table as well. If you’re unsure where we are, just a 📅 Every Thursday, 7:30pm (You’re welcome to arrive earlier or later) 🍻 This isn’t a sober event, but there’s no pressure to drink. The bar offers zero-alcohol options as well as tea and coffee

title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-19 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. input.evidence_text: Shutterstock | Fletcher Moss Shutterstock A local’s guide to Didsbury Village, Manchester’s cutest neighbourhood Discover the very best places to eat, drink and be merry in Didsbury Village Share Copy Link Facebook Twitter Pinterest Email WhatsApp Written by Genevieve Stevens Contributing writer, UK Advertising window.rawSlots = window.rawSlots || []; window.rawSlots.push('{"adSlotId":"/5185/body1/feature","adContainerId":"ad-body-1-8277","isOutOfPage":false,"isRegi Didsbury is locally known as one of the more shi-shi of South Manchester’s suburbs. Whilst nearby Fallowfield and Withington cater to a grungier student crowd, Didsbury services a mixed bag of young professionals, families, retirees and, of course, their fashionable dogs- almost If you’re after cafes, Didsbury has them in droves. title: It Walks Around The House At Night

input.lead: It Walks Around The House At Night
input.summary: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside m
input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

The Brazil international’s move to Old Trafford was thrown into doubt earlier this week after medical tests flagged up issu It is far from the first time a proposed United signing has hit a major stumbling block during a medical. Club legend Ruud van Nistelrooy knows that feeling better than most, having joined United from Dutch side PSV Eindhoven in 2001 But his tenure with United was actually supposed to start a year earlier. Speaking on The Overlap with Gary Neville in 2003, the former Netherlands striker recalled the extraordinary ordeal “My first attempt signing for Manchester United was the most anxious moment in my career,” he told his former team-mate. “I did the whole medical in Manchester, and we were driving up to Old Trafford to do the press conference to sign and the phone rang “I was told by the orthopaedic surgeon from United who saw the MRI that my knee was not right. He said, ‘you have to turn around and go back to your hotel, you’re not going to pass a medical’

title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! title: Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West

input.evidence_text: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development A 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could cut journey times between Manchester and Sheffield by 30 minutes, according to an ambitious proposal A Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead The Woodhead railway line between the major northern cities would also be restored under the plans. The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 This scheme, named 'Trans-Pennine Connect', aims to make transport between south Yorkshire and the north west better. By putting that traffic underground, they say they can hand the Peak District landscape back to nature This in turn, they say, could boost the productivity of the region and inject millions into the local economy. input.evidence_text: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0 Manchester United have confirmed the club's new 100,000-seater stadium will be built on land recently purchased from Indurent, situated roughly 350 metres from Old Trafford United confirmed the location for the new stadium on Thursday in the Wharfside Strategic Masterplan and while the stadium won't be built on land currently connected to Old Trafford, they insist it will honour the current ground's history and traditions The new ground will be the centrepiece of a Stadium District, which will be purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity, with the new stadium serving as the flagship landmark of the wider Trafford Wharfside development The masterplan sets out a bold vision for Trafford Wharfside, including new and improved public transport links, enhanced rail connectivity, and extensive walking and cycling infrastructure. The vision is for a diverse neighbourhood creating 48,000 local jobs and 15,000 new homes It is estimated it could offer a £7.3bn per year boost to the UK economy, reports the Manchester Evening News

title: Rudy's to open in Monton Salford - About Manchester

input.lead: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn.
input.summary: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a
input.evidence_text: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a The new pizzeria, located at 237-239 Monton Road , will transform the former Bennetts Furnishers store that has been vacant since 2022, with work now officially underway. The spacious venue will mark Rudy’s 41st restaurant to date Since launching in Manchester’s Ancoats in 2015, Rudy’s has grown into one of the UK’s most loved pizzerias, celebrated for its light, double fermented dough and unwavering dedication to true Neopolitan craft Testament to its authenticity, Rudy’s is the largest organisation in the world to be accredited by the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN), the international body that protects and promotes traditional Neapolitan pizza making Rudy’s has also been recognised among the World’s 50 Best Pizzeria, ranking 23rd globally for 2025. Each pizza, from the classic Margherita to the fiery Calabrese, is made to AVPN standards using traditional techniques and ingredients sourced directly from Italy To claim one of 3,000 complimentary pizzas, Monton locals can sign up via this link , where they will be able to choose from Rudy’s favourites such as its Cinghiale , Margherita Con Bufala, and Triple Pepperoni to enjoy at the new opening this summer
